settings.model_hint: Select an AI review model per repo. Haiku → fast & cheap ($0.80/1M) · Sonnet → balanced ($3/1M) · Opus → high quality ($15/1M). Leave empty to use the server global default (claude-sonnet-4-6). ※ Prices are based on Anthropic official rates (actual billing may differ).

onboarding.intro: Complete the steps below to receive analysis results.
onboarding.step1: ① Configure a notification channel → Connect Telegram or another channel from the Outbound Channels card below
onboarding.step2: ② Choose review level → Select a preset under Quick Settings above (optional)

webhook_stale.title: Webhook reinstall required
webhook_stale.desc: Auto-merge retry may be delayed: check_suite event is not subscribed. Reinstalling the webhook enables immediate retries on CI completion.

preset_card.desc: Expanding a card auto-applies its settings. Verify and click the Save button to commit. Notification channel URLs (Slack, Discord etc.) are not modified.
preset_card.channel_unchanged: This preset does not modify notification channel URLs.

tenants.subtitle: SaaS multi-tenant usage — read-only / This page is admin only
tenants.empty_hint: Tenants appear automatically once a user signs in via GitHub OAuth.
tenants.info_li1: This page is read-only — user data cannot be modified
tenants.info_li2: When kill-switch is active (`SAAS_MULTITENANT_DISABLED=1`), this page returns 503
tenants.info_li4: Phase 2 area (billing / usage caps / per-tenant API key) = separate PR required

rls_audit.subtitle: Supabase PostgreSQL Row Level Security matrix — static / alembic migration results
rls_audit.info_li1: RLS applied = PostgreSQL only (SQLite unit tests auto-skip)
rls_audit.info_li3: contextvars bridge = `src/shared/rls_context.py` + DB event listener (`SET LOCAL app.user_id`)
rls_audit.info_li4: 2-layer safety = (1) app-level filter (`_apply_*_user_filter`) + (2) DB RLS policy (PG/Supabase) — ⚠️ without FORCE, an owner connection can bypass (2)
rls_audit.force_warning_title: ⚠️ FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY not set — 2nd safety layer may be bypassed
rls_audit.force_warning_body: FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY is not confirmed on all 11 RLS tables (live pg_class check — alembic 0041 not applied / partially applied, or a non-PostgreSQL environment). If the app connection role owns the tables, PostgreSQL bypasses RLS for the owner, so despite the 'applied' matrix above the DB-level 2nd safety layer may not actually take effect. Verify `alembic upgrade head` and the switch to the non-BYPASSRLS app role (docs/runbooks/rls-role-separation.md).
rls_audit.bypass_warning_title: ⚠️ Connection role bypasses RLS — 2nd safety layer ineffective despite FORCE
rls_audit.bypass_warning_body: FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY is applied on all 11 tables, but the current connection role is BYPASSRLS or superuser, so RLS is never evaluated (live pg_roles.rolbypassrls/rolsuper check). The DB-level 2nd safety layer stays ineffective until DATABASE_URL switches to the non-BYPASSRLS app role (Phase 4 — docs/runbooks/rls-role-separation.md).

operations.subtitle: 5 KPIs (cache / API cost / merge / pipeline latency) — admin only / in-memory counters (reset on process restart)
operations.latency_phase2_pending: 📭 Phase 2 area — in-memory counter infrastructure not yet present
operations.i18n_fallback_hint: Higher fallback rate = more missing translations — operator action required (resets on process restart)
operations.info_li1: In-memory counter limit — resets on Railway redeploy (cache stats / api_cost). DB persist = Phase 2 area
operations.info_li2: silent fallback streak — 5 consecutive cache misses trigger logger WARNING (Railway logs)
operations.info_li3: 1-week baseline report = Cycle 81+ separate work (option C — cross-verify NEW-P0-3 alignment)

errors.issue_no_write_permission: No write permission for Issues. Please check your GitHub token.
errors.invalid_url: Invalid URL: {field}. Internal network addresses are not allowed.
errors.repo_access_denied: You do not have GitHub access to this repository

merge_advice.branch_protection_blocked: Branch Protection Rules requirements not met — complete the required reviewer approval or required CI checks.
merge_advice.dirty_conflict: Conflict with the base branch — run `git pull origin main`, resolve the conflicts, and push again.
merge_advice.behind_base: Behind the base branch — click the 'Update branch' button on the PR page to sync.
merge_advice.draft_pr: Draft PRs are not eligible for auto-merge — switch to 'Ready for review' and push again.
merge_advice.unstable_ci: CI checks are failing or still running — try again after all required status checks pass.
merge_advice.unknown_state_timeout: GitHub's mergeable state calculation did not finish — refresh the PR page after a moment and retry.
merge_advice.permission_denied: The GitHub token lacks `pull_requests: write` permission — check the Fine-grained PAT or GitHub App permissions.
merge_advice.not_mergeable: The GitHub API responded that the PR is not mergeable — check the PR page for the detailed reason.
merge_advice.unprocessable: Merge conditions were not satisfied (422) — check all of the PR's checks and protection rules.
merge_advice.conflict_sha_changed: The head SHA changed (409) — if another push went first, it will retry automatically on the next push.
merge_advice.network_error: GitHub API network error — this may be temporary. Try merging directly on the PR page.
merge_advice.auto_merge_disabled_in_repo_settings: The repo Settings → General → 'Allow auto-merge' toggle is OFF. Enable it in the GitHub repo settings and native auto-merge will work from the next PR.
merge_advice.enable_permission_denied: The GitHub token lacks GraphQL `enablePullRequestAutoMerge` permission. Check the `repo` scope on the OAuth user token or the GitHub App `pull_requests: write` permission.
merge_advice.force_pushed: The PR head SHA differs from when it was enabled, so a force-push was detected. When the new SHA arrives, re-analysis and retry run automatically (no user action needed).
merge_advice.enable_api_error: Failed to enable native auto-merge due to a GitHub GraphQL API error. Click the 'Enable auto-merge' button directly on the PR page, or retry after a moment.
merge_advice.default: Check the merge conditions on the PR page and take the necessary action.

github_pr_comment.title: ## {emoji} SCAManager Analysis Result
github_pr_comment.static_incomplete_warning: > ⚠️ **Static analysis incomplete (timeout or error)** — the score below may be inflated and is not reliable. Review manually before merging.

gate.retry_stopped_reason: Retry stopped: auto_merge disabled or threshold changed.

railway.log_fetch_failed: Failed to fetch logs. Please check the Railway dashboard.

landing.error_oauth_failed: There was a problem signing in. Please try again.
landing.error_generic: A temporary error occurred. Please try again in a moment.
landing.hero_sub: Automatic GitHub / GitLab integration with Telegram alerts<br class="br-md">for complete code quality management
landing.demo_review_1: Security: JWT token validation logic is solid. HMAC-SHA256 usage confirmed.
landing.demo_review_2: Performance: get_current_user queries the DB on every request. Consider a Redis cache.
landing.demo_review_3: Security: Session cookie is missing the HttpOnly flag. Risk of XSS exposure.
landing.demo_review_4: Code Quality: Function separation and the single-responsibility principle are well observed.
landing.feature_auto_desc: Static analysis + AI review the moment you push. No waiting for feedback.
landing.feature_gate_desc: Score-based automatic PR approval/rejection. Sub-threshold code is blocked automatically.
landing.feature_score_desc: A clear 100-point code quality score. Grasp it at a glance with grades.
landing.feature_notify_desc: Telegram, Discord, Slack, Email, n8n. Receive on the channel you want.
